entry.title: a station over
entry.summary:
Author: capricious_Bastard

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,348Chapters: 1/2Kudos: 0

Summary: “Hello, daddy,” the word is hissed out as the person stands in front of him, blocking his view. Blinking and focusing on his new companion, he resists the urge to lean back in surprise. “Hey, baby.”

entry.title: The Physics of Familiarity
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 8,351Chapters: 4/11Kudos: 15

Summary: Lois Lane knows Clark Kent the way you know a city you've lived in for twelve years—by which streets flood in heavy rain, which shortcuts only work on Tuesdays, which silences mean danger and which mean he's just watching a bird. Alfred Pennyworth has been recording Bruce Wayne's vital signs, dietary intake, psychological indicators, and patrol efficiency for thirty years. He has documentation. He has baselines. He has thirty years of knowing exactly what this man looks like. Neither of them recognizes what's sitting across from them at breakfast. At Tuesday lunch. In the study at 6 AM in Thomas Wayne's Edinburgh sweater, holding coffee with both hands, watching the garden like it's somewhere he's allowed to be. They came to the same conclusion by different routes: something is wrong. They're beginning to suspect they might be wrong about that.

entry.title: Bruce and Clark
entry.summary:
Author: TS_Rhodes

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,117Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 20

Summary: When ten-year-old Bruce Wayne's ride breaks down near the Kent farm, he meets ten-year-old Clark, and a friendship forms. The Kents are concerned about Bruce, since he is recently orphaned. Clark, whose powers are just starting to come in, pleads to keep Bruce as a friend. So they invite Bruce and his guardian Alfred to come back and stay the summer. And so it begins...

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne
  • Childhood Friends

entry.title: Two Months In Kansas
entry.summary:
Author: Superbat_fan02

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 8,403Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 41

Summary: Alfred forces Bruce to spend two months on vacation in Kansas, a small, sparsely populated town, to give him a break from his grueling training and the mental exhaustion of spending almost every night at his computer reading thousands of Gotham police reports. On this short trip, Bruce meets Clark, a boy a year younger than him who is his complete opposite. Clark is cheerful, honest, empathetic, and responsible. The golden boy of Kansas, always with a smile and a pleasant tone of voice. But Bruce doesn’t believe that’s the whole story. Clark lives on and grew up on the farm where there are reports of a UFO crash, and, mysteriously, his “parents”—who weren’t parents at the time—are now his parents. Was it really a coincidence that Clark appeared after this supposed UFO news? Furthermore, not to mention that Bruce was already considered tall and well-built for his age, Clark was even taller and bigger than him. He always seemed to hear more than he intended to, or would carelessly lift objects that would be a challenge even for adults to lift. Bruce was sure of it—there was something about Clark, and he wanted to find out what it was.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Missing (bat)Man
entry.summary:
Author: LadyRiversoftheNorth

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,190Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 30

Summary: This story is a Superbat story inspired by the manhua Missing Love: The Marrying Man. I treat cannon as my own personal playground so there will be some bending where I want to because I am god here. Unlike my other work, this will not have an update schedule, I apologize. Chapters will come as inspiration does, but I do not foresee it being more than a few weeks between each update. TW: Graphic descriptions of torture, violence, and rape in this fiction, (never between the ML and MC!)   When Bruce/Batman gets in over his head and is kidnapped, Superman must step in for Gotham, and for Bruce's ward/sidekick Dick Grayson. But as Clark Kent he must also track down Bruce Wayne...and he is not the world's greatest detective.

entry.title: Blue Bird's Wings
entry.summary:
Author: InfinityBlaze

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,275Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 5

Summary: Dick Grayson was on the trail of something hot, and he knew it. A fringe cult that had started up recently and had made moves into Gotham, using force to take space in the abandoned corners of the city, reportedly using some kind of magic to do so. Alongside this there seemed to be an opposing cult that was trying to destroy this other faction. Dick wanted to be on top of this new threat to the city, and whilst on the cusp of a breakthrough was captured by the secondary cult on suspicions of him being a “Witch”. This catches the dark eyes of a being just starting to gain back his power.

entry.title: Homecoming
entry.summary:
Author: eosthedawn (futurepilot)

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,608Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 10

Summary: Clark chases after Bruce with the hope that together, they can investigate the way the meteor has changed their lives, and that they can voice this unspoken thing between them that's gone unspoken for too long. Maybe they can finally spend time together after a mission. Maybe he's finally gotten his hopes up too high. Or, directly after Public Enemies.

entry.title: Do I Wanna Know?
entry.summary:
Author: Deluv_011

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 68,154Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 473

Summary: Damian Wayne and Conner Kent form an unexpected couple. However, few knew that behind that façade, each of them had their heart burning for someone else. Thus, Damian and Conner cling to each other as a distorted reflection of what they truly long for. Damian yearns for Jon Kent. Conner pines for Tim Drake. And the deeper they fall into that strange relationship, the more they begin to notice certain glances among their teammates… glances that reveal far more than mere friendship.

entry.title: like grey skies (like a bird of paradise)
entry.summary:
Author: rainbow_nerds

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 41,078Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 356

Summary: He sighed to himself and downed the glass of champagne he'd been nursing—non-alcoholic, but enough to convince anyone who might give him a passing glance. He hated this, every time he had to do it. There was one surefire way to derail anyone who began wondering whether maybe Bruce Wayne was doing more than just donating funds to Batman.His tie hung loose around his neck, shirt unbuttoned to halfway down his chest, laughing too easily, pressing close to everyone he spoke to, whispering in ears, playing the part, until he turned and froze on the spot.Clark Kent, in his signature ill-fitting suit, stood holding a notebook and pen, staring at him with barely-concealed glee.Bruce cringed internally. He hated this. He especially hated when it had to be witnessed by someone he actually respected.or: After a Gala, Bruce and Clark end up in bed together. They're friends, just helping each other out, no strings attached. Which would be great, if Bruce hadn't been halfway to head-over-heels since the day they met.

entry.title: i will listen, tell it all (when you're finished, we'll talk more)
entry.summary:
Author: snapdragonsuplex

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,466Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 170

Summary: “Bruce?”He turns his head to look at the receptionist.“Dr. Quinzel is ready for you, if you’re ready to go in.”Bruce nods, then stands up, putting his hands in his hoodie’s pockets as he walks over to the door leading to the offices. He takes a breath, then walks through, glancing around until he finds the door labelled “Harleen Quinzel, PsyD.”He hesitates, then, after reminding himself why he’s doing this, knocks on her door.----------------Bruce goes to therapy for the first time since he was eight years old.

entry.title: In plain sight - podfic
entry.summary:
Author: NovInked

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 4Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 10

Summary: Speaking Kryptonese under his breath had always been Clark Kent’s favorite way to let off steam. No one on Earth understood it, so he could vent, insult his teammates, and maybe flirt outrageously with Batman without consequence. At least, that’s what he thought… until Batman casually answered him back in flawless Kryptonese.

entry.title: Shadows Run Deep
entry.summary:
Author: serephent

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,476Chapters: 2/5Kudos: 12

Summary: Bruce Wayne’s life is imploding. His parents were criminals. His company belongs to Penguin. Harvey Dent is spiraling. And the Children of Arkham are dragging Gotham into chaos. Batman is trying to hold the line. Gotham isn’t making it easy. (A superbat reimagining of Telltale Batman. It doesn’t follow the game exactly because: Clark is there. Laws exist. Bruce isn't an idiot.)

entry.title: Nolan’s Dark Knight v the Man of Steel
entry.summary:
Author: legobiwan

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 73,420Chapters: 32/?Kudos: 21

Summary: Three years after the battle with Bane a tragedy accuses on the invasion of Zod which draws Bruce Wayne to return to Gotham along with Alfred and Robin (John Blake). Batman starts planning to capture Superman for his part in the Metropolis destruction, no matter how dark his methods are getting, but Lex Luthor partners with the Joker in their plan to turn The Dark Knight against The Man Of Steel.

entry.title: Candle Against The Wind
entry.summary:
Author: LoneChestnutTree

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 14,512Chapters: 3/4Kudos: 24

Summary: 'The flurry of wind does not snuff the flame from the candle, despite. But a stronger wind blows, and it does. The air is full. Something stronger than him and his misery. He does not lift his head. To his right, a figure by the archway.  Suddenly, his kneeling reverence made sense.'Bruce gets injured. Clark visits. Winter arrives. And they are in the middle of it.

entry.title: Don't Dream it's Over
entry.summary:
Author: NightTwink73

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 19,459Chapters: 7/20Kudos: 39

Summary: Eight months after Clark and Lois’s divorce, a breakup letter appears on Commissioner Gordon’s desk—addressed to Batman. But everything about it is wrong. Dear Batman,I can’t do this anymore. Whatever this is between us—it isn’t fair to either of us. You’ve always kept me at a distance, and I told myself I was okay with that, but I’m not.I need something real. Something you were never willing to give me.Don’t try to find me. I’m safe, and I won’t be coming back.I love you. Goodbye.— Superman As Bruce investigates, it quickly becomes clear: maybe the letter isn’t lying about Clark not coming back—and that’s a terrifying thought. Meanwhile, Clark wakes up in a dream life, living beside the man he’s never dared to love… trapped in a prison disguised as perfection.

entry.title: Two Heroes and a Baby
entry.summary:
Author: xxEmilee_Lunaxx

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 104,423Chapters: 29/?Kudos: 550

Summary: In this AU, 19-year-old Bruce Wayne adopts 4-year-old Dick Grayson after he is orphaned by a tragic event at Haly's Circus. Bruce is in over his head trying to juggle establishing himself as a responsible and valuable asset to Wayne Enterprises, beginning his vigilante work in Gotham as The Batman, and parenting a young child despite not knowing where to start. After struggling to get good press and constantly being seen as an irresponsible nepo baby, he sets up an interview at The Daily Planet. That's where he meets 19-year-old intern Clark Kent, who is eager to write a good story to begin his career as a journalist. After Clark agrees to keep an eye on Dick during his interview, Bruce realizes he has a knack for watching children, especially young Dick Grayson. Bruce asks Clark to come live with him at Wayne Manor on a 6 month assignment as Dick's nanny, and in return, will let Clark write the story of a lifetime--an expose on his life that no other reporter has even come close to. Clark reluctantly agrees, but is hiding a secret from Bruce--his life as the superhero, Superman! As the two begin their friendship and double lives as heroes, they learn valuable lessons in friendship, love, and parenting.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
